title: fix: remove unused issue automation backfill
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Removes the one-off Issue Automation Backfill workflow.
- Deletes 296 lines of duplicated triage logic that has not run since April 11.
- Leaves the active issue automation workflow unchanged.
- Confirms no repository references depend on the backfill workflow.

gist.headline: Backfill leaves the garden
gist.blurb: A retired one-off triage workflow has been composted. The active issue automations keep tending the queue, minus 296 lines of dormant duplicate logic.
gist.summary: Removed the unused Issue Automation Backfill GitHub workflow, deleting 296 lines of duplicate triage logic while leaving the active automation unchanged.
gist.impact: Repository maintenance is simpler with no behavior change to the active issue-triage workflow.
gist.visual_concept: Depict Polly as a tidy gardener removing an old, clearly retired automation scroll from a cozy garden shed while a nearby active CRT terminal continues watering healthy code-plants; the old backfill is being composted, not treated as a failure.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly, the cute yellow-and-brown striped bee mascot with pink cheek blush, light blue wings, and a tiny gardener hat, gently placing an old scroll labeled "BACKFILL" into a compost bin while a retro CRT terminal keeps watering flourishing code-plants. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic with a prominent lime green (#ecf874) glow, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, subtle scanlines, and retro gaming vibes; a small flower garden makes the cleanup feel calm and useful.

Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic with a prominent lime green (#ecf874) glow, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, subtle scanlines, and retro gaming vibes; a small flower garden makes the cleanup feel calm and useful.
